description: In a clinic examination room, Ross stands next to Monica, who sits on an exam table. Ross has his arm around her shoulders while she holds her hands clasped together. Ross says, "Sorry."
Ross briefly lowers his head while Monica continues looking away.
Ross turns his head to his right, and says, "Hey, I got my answers back," his expression brightening. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross turns his gaze back toward Monica, his expression returning to neutral.
Ross says, "which we can celebrate later." Monica shifts her gaze away from him without responding, and audience laughter sounds.
The scene changes to a city street outside the "Central Perk" coffee shop. Phoebe stands on the sidewalk, holding an acoustic guitar.
Phoebe strums her guitar and sings, "What's a double double double jointed?"
Phoebe sings, "Boy," while a woman with short dark hair, Stephanie, carrying a guitar case, steps into view.
Stephanie stops near Phoebe as the audience cheers. Phoebe stops playing, holds her guitar, and looks at her.
Phoebe asks, "hey, so, are you the professional guitar player?" Stephanie nods and replies, "Yeah."
Phoebe asks, "Bethany, right?"
Stephanie says, "Yeah. My name was on there, but now it just says carrot cake," gesturing slightly toward a sign. The audience laughs.
Phoebe looks toward a nearby A-frame sign that displays the words "Carrot Cake," then turns back to Stephanie.
Phoebe asks, "How many chords do you know?"
Stephanie nods and says, "All of them." Phoebe raises her eyebrows and asks, "Oh, yeah? So you know d"
Stephanie answers, "yeah." Phoebe asks, "Okay. Do you know a minor?"
Stephanie nods slightly.
Stephanie says, "Yeah." Phoebe responds, "Okay."
Phoebe leans forward slightly and asks, "Do you know how to go from D to A minor?"
Stephanie looks down momentarily, then looks back up.
Stephanie says, "Yeah."
Phoebe nods slowly.
Phoebe says, "Okay."
Phoebe repositions her hands on her guitar.
Phoebe asks, "So, does your guitar have a strap?"
Stephanie shakes her head.
Stephanie says, "No," and Phoebe immediately adds, "mine does," prompting audience laughter.
Stephanie places her guitar case on the ground. Phoebe resumes strumming her guitar.
Phoebe sings, "Stephanie all the chords," while strumming.
The scene changes to a city intersection where Chandler stands at a public payphone, holding the receiver. Joey stands next to him carrying an empty baby carrier strapped to his chest.
Chandler holds the phone to his ear and says, "Come on. Pick up, pick up, pick up, pick up."
Chandler says into the phone, "Hello? Transit Authority. Yes, hello." Joey looks at him.
Joey pats Chandler's shoulder.
Chandler continues speaking into the phone, "I'm doing research for a book and I was wondering what somebody might do if they left the baby on a city bus."
Chandler listens, his facial expression dropping slightly.
Chandler says into the phone, "Yes, I do realize that'd be a very stupid character," followed by audience laughter. Joey reaches over and pulls the phone from Chandler's hand.
Joey speaks into the receiver, "Hi. Here's the deal. We lost a car seat on a bus today. It's white, plastic"
Joey continues, "with a handle, and it fits onto a stroller. Oh, and there was a baby in it."
Joey listens to the receiver, then lowers the phone slightly.
Joey turns to Chandler and says, "Wants to talk to you again." The audience laughs.
Chandler takes the phone back and puts it to his ear. The scene changes to the interior of Central Perk, where Rachel stands at a microphone stand in front of a seated audience.
Rachel speaks into the microphone, "Okay, everybody, let's give a nice, warm Central Perk welcome."
Outside the window, Phoebe strums her guitar and sings loudly, "Erase a jerk and he won't let me work and I hate"
Phoebe finishes her sung phrase, "Central Perk," and the audience laughs.
Rachel looks back toward the window, then faces the crowd again and steps closer to the microphone.
Rachel says, "To Stephanie schiffer," and claps her hands.
The crowd applauds. Stephanie steps up to the microphone, holding her acoustic guitar, while Rachel steps aside.
Stephanie adjusts the microphone stand and positions her hands on her guitar.
Stephanie speaks into the microphone, introducing her song.
Stephanie strums her guitar and sings, "Zachary"
Outside the window, Phoebe continues strumming her guitar.
Phoebe sings loudly, "invited to bite me"
Inside Central Perk, several patrons turn their heads toward the window, looking in Phoebe's direction.

The scene changes to a building exterior showing a sign that reads "CITY OF NEW YORK DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES." The scene then shifts indoors to an office space where Chandler and Joey rush through a doorway, carrying pieces of a stroller.
Chandler says loudly, "about the baby. We lost the baby," as Joey stands beside him. Audience laughter sounds.
A man wearing a light blue shirt and tie stands up behind a desk, looking at them.
Chandler and Joey stop at the desk. Chandler holds pieces of a yellow and blue stroller. Joey wears the empty baby carrier strapped to his chest.
The man looks at them and says, "I'm assuming one of you is the father." Chandler gestures toward Joey and says, "That's me."
Joey turns his head toward Chandler, then looks back at the man.
Chandler says, "Actually,"
Chandler raises his arm and rests his hand on Joey's shoulder. Joey places his hand on Chandler's back.
Chandler says, "we're both the father," followed by loud audience laughter.
The man stares silently at them. Chandler and Joey hold their pose, standing close together.
The scene changes to a nursery room filled with cribs. A woman wearing a green cardigan walks out through a doorway. Chandler and Joey rush into the room.
Joey and Chandler separate and walk between the cribs. Joey leans forward to look inside one of the cribs.
Chandler leans over another crib, then stands up straight, his eyes widening as he looks around the room.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Please tell me you know which one is our baby," prompting audience laughter.
Joey stares downward toward the cribs without responding.
In a nursery room filled with cribs, Chandler stands beside Joey, who wears an empty baby carrier strapped to his chest. Both men look downward at the cribs.
Pointing at one crib and then another, Joey says, "Well, that one has ducks on his T shirt and this one has clowns."
Joey lowers his arm and turns his gaze toward Chandler.
"And Ben was definitely wearing ducks. Or clowns," Joey says, his voice trailing off as Chandler turns to look at him.
Audience laughter is heard. Joey gasps and extends his arm, pointing directly toward one of the cribs.
Joey leans over the crib and says, "Oh, wait, that one's definitely Ben."
Joey says, "Remember, he had that cute little mole by his yeah. Yeah." Chandler nods and moves to lean over the crib alongside Joey.
Reaching his hand down into the crib, Chandler says softly, "Hey, Ben. Remember us?"
Chandler's facial expression falls as he pulls his hand back, pinching a small dark object between his fingers, and stands up straight.
Chandler holds the small object up and says to Joey, "Okay, the mole came off."
Joey slaps Chandler's arm and steps back, his eyes widening as audience laughter plays.
Chandler looks at Joey and asks, "What are we gonna do?"
Joey echoes, "What are we gonna do?"
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "Uh, we'll flip for it. Ducks or clowns." Chandler steps forward and says, "Oh, we're going to flip for the baby."
"You got a better idea? All right, call it in the air," Joey says, reaching into his pocket and pulling out a coin.
Joey tosses the coin upward.
As the coin falls back into Joey's hands, Chandler says, "Heads."
Joey looks at his palm and says, "Heads it is. Yes." He cheers and claps Chandler on the shoulder.
Chandler grabs the straps of Joey's baby carrier, his eyes widening as he shakes Joey slightly. Audience laughter builds.
Chandler says, "We have to assign heads to something."
Joey's smile drops as he stares back at Chandler.
Joey nods once and asks, "Right?"
Joey adds, "Okay," raising his hands to his forehead.
Joey rubs his temples with both hands, looking down. Audience laughter continues.
Lowering his hands, Joey states, "Ducks? Heads, because ducks have heads."
Chandler stares at Joey, keeping his jaw rigid. Audience laughter continues.
Chandler leans slightly forward and says, "Kind of scary ass clowns came to your birthday?"

Chandler and Joey turn their attention back to the cribs. The scene changes to the sidewalk outside a storefront with a "CENTRAL PERK" window sign, where Phoebe, wearing a purple jacket, sits on the curb beside an open guitar case.
The setting changes to the interior of Central Perk. Stephanie sits on a stool, playing an acoustic guitar and singing into a microphone, while Jim sits on a nearby couch listening.
Stephanie sings, "Not if my love can't bind your heart"
Stephanie continues strumming her guitar as the angle shifts to show her from the side.
Stephanie sings, "and there's no need to take a stand"
She continues singing, "for it was I who chose to start."
Stephanie strums her guitar rhythmically.
Stephanie sings, "I see no reason to take me home."
She continues playing the guitar as Jim listens in the background.
Stephanie sings, "I'm old enough to face the dawn"
A woman in a light grey collared shirt walks into view holding a red mug and stops.
Stephanie sings, "just call me angel of the morning angel" as the woman in the grey shirt watches her.
She continues, "just touch my cheek before" while maintaining her strumming rhythm.
Stephanie sings, "you leave me, baby"
She continues, "just call me angel of the morning"
Stephanie sings, "angel then slowly turn away" while looking forward.
She continues playing the acoustic guitar.
Stephanie sings, "I won't beg you to stay"
Stephanie pauses vocally, keeping her hands on the guitar.
She finishes the lyric, singing, "with me"
Stephanie strums her guitar steadily.
Stephanie sings, "maybe the sun's light. Will be dim"
She continues, "so it won't matter anyhow"
Stephanie continues playing and singing, with Jim remaining seated on the couch behind her.
Stephanie sings, "if Morning's Echo says we've seen"
She sings, "well, it was what I wanted now"
The woman in the grey shirt lowers her gaze slightly. The scene changes back to the sidewalk outside, where Phoebe holds and counts cash in her hands.
Stephanie's singing continues audibly: "and if I victims of the night" as Phoebe finishes counting the money.
Phoebe folds the cash and places it into her jacket pocket.
Stephanie's singing continues: "I won't be blinded by the light" as the scene changes back to the woman in the grey shirt inside the coffee shop.
The woman in the grey shirt stands holding her red mug while Stephanie's singing and guitar playing continue audibly.
Stephanie sings clearly into the microphone as Jim watches her from the couch.
Stephanie continues her song, strumming the guitar rhythmically.
The woman in the grey shirt maintains her gaze toward Stephanie.
The scene changes to an outdoor payphone. Chandler holds the black receiver to his ear and speaks into it while looking downward.
Inside Central Perk, Stephanie sits playing an acoustic guitar and singing into a microphone. She wears a black sleeveless top.
Stephanie continues singing, "through the tears," as multiple overlapping views of her performance are shown.
A man with dreadlocks sits nearby. Stephanie sings, "of the pain."
Stephanie continues to strum the guitar, singing, "of the years."
Stephanie's volume rises as she repeatedly sings, "baby," strumming the guitar rhythmically.
Stephanie continues singing, her face close to the microphone.
Stephanie sits on a tall stool, continuing her song.
Stephanie continues playing the guitar.
Stephanie sings, "just call me angel of the morning angel."
Rachel sits on a couch holding a red mug, as Stephanie sings, "just touch my cheek before."
Stephanie continues singing, "you leave me baby, baby all baby."
Rachel stands up from the couch and walks toward the glass door.
Outside the coffee shop, Phoebe sits on the curb next to her open guitar case, holding money. Rachel exits the shop and walks over to her.
Phoebe looks up at Rachel and says, "Oh, hi."
Rachel stands in front of Phoebe, holding her red mug and a cloth.
Rachel holds out the cloth to Phoebe, saying, "Here. I thought you might be cold." Phoebe takes it and says, "Thank you."
Rachel places her red mug on the curb next to the guitar case, while Phoebe places the cloth on her lap.
Rachel straightens up and says, "Um."
Rachel bends her knees and squats down beside Phoebe. Both women look at the money in Phoebe's hands.
Rachel looks at the money and says, "wow. Look at you. You did pretty well."
Phoebe counts the bills and says, "$8.27."
Phoebe looks away briefly, then turns her gaze back to Rachel.
Phoebe gestures with the money and says, "But not really, because I put in the first two".
Phoebe pauses briefly.
Phoebe continues, "just to get the ball rolling and to make myself feel better," gesturing with her hand.
Rachel tilts her head and asks, "Do you?"
Phoebe looks at Rachel.
Phoebe shakes her head slightly and says, "No."
Phoebe lowers her gaze back to the money.
Phoebe looks at Rachel and says, "This whole, like, playing for money thing is so not good for me."
Phoebe shakes her head, flipping her hair back over her shoulder.
Phoebe looks away and says, "I don't know, when I sang Susu Suicide,"
Rachel keeps her gaze steady on Phoebe.
Phoebe turns to Rachel and says, "I got a dollar 75."
Phoebe pauses.
Phoebe says, "But then Smelly Cat".
Rachel nods slightly, continuing to watch Phoebe.
Phoebe says, "I got twenty five cents and a condom." Rachel opens her mouth slightly in reaction.
Rachel keeps her eyes widened slightly as she looks at Phoebe.
Phoebe shakes her head and says, "So now I just feel really bad for Smelly Cat."
Phoebe looks down at her hands. Rachel shifts her posture slightly.
Rachel looks at Phoebe and says, "Well,".
Rachel gestures briefly with her hand.
Rachel says, "I don't think everybody gets Smelly Cat".
Rachel pauses.
Rachel continues, "if all you've ever actually had are healthy pets and whoosh," making a sweeping hand gesture on "whoosh."
Phoebe nods in agreement.
Phoebe looks at Rachel and says, "But it's not even that."
Phoebe shakes her head slightly.
Phoebe touches her chest and says, "I don't know. I used to do my songs because it made me happy."
Phoebe continues, "Now it's just all about the money," gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe tosses the money into her guitar case. Rachel places her hand on Phoebe's knee.
Rachel says, "Well, people missed you in there." Phoebe looks at her.
Rachel continues, "And in fact, there was actually a request for Smelly Cat." Phoebe immediately asks, "Really?"
Phoebe leans forward slightly, asking, "From who?"
Rachel smiles softly and replies, "Well, me."
Phoebe smiles back at Rachel.
Rachel continues, "And I know that's not your big money song, but it's my favorite," gesturing with both hands.
Phoebe smiles. A young man wearing a striped shirt and an open jacket runs out of the coffee shop toward them.
The young man stops, panting slightly, and asks Phoebe, "Hi. Did I accidentally drop a condom in your case?"
Phoebe points at her guitar case. The young man bends down, reaches into the case, and retrieves the item.
The young man holds up the item and says, "It's kind of an emergency," before turning to run back toward the coffee shop.
Phoebe watches the young man leave.
Outside the coffee shop, Phoebe and Rachel sit on the curb next to Phoebe's open guitar case. The young man from the previous segment stands in front of them, holding the retrieved item.
The young man drops a coin into the guitar case and says, "Here you go. Hey, thanks a lot." He runs down the street, shouting, "Hey, Christine. I got it." Phoebe and Rachel watch him go.
The setting changes to the exterior of a corner apartment building with a red awning over the ground floor.
Now inside an apartment living room, Ross and Monica sit together on a beige sofa, facing each other.
Ross looks at Monica and says, "I just want to thank you for being there for me today."
Ross maintains his gaze on Monica.
Ross continues, "And I'm sorry I almost broke your hand." Monica replies, "That's okay," while gently rubbing her hands together.
Monica, still rubbing her hands, says, "I'm sorry I poisoned you." Ross nods.
Ross looks at Monica, a slight smile on his face.
Ross says, "Remember the time I jammed that pencil into your hand?" while making a downward stabbing gesture with one hand.
Monica looks down at her hand and says, "Remember it?"
Monica holds her hand out toward Ross, pointing to a spot on her palm.
Monica points to her palm and asks, "What do you think? This?"
Ross leans back slightly, looking at her hand, and says, "Oh." Monica points her index finger at Ross.
Monica points her finger at Ross and asks, "What about a time that I hit you in the face with Sylvie's pumpkin?"
Ross leans forward, gesturing with a closed fist, and says, "Oh, man. Well, remember when I stuck that broom in your bike spokes"
Ross makes a circular flipping motion with his hand and says, "and you flipped over and hit your head on the curb?"
Monica shakes her head slightly and says, "No, but I remember people telling me about it."
Ross smiles, briefly looking away before returning his gaze to Monica.
Ross gestures toward Monica with an open hand and says, "Well, Ben has a little sister."
Monica smiles broadly and replies, "Yeah, and I hope she can kick his ass." Ross laughs and crosses his arms.
Ross continues smiling with his arms crossed.
Ross says, "I'm going to get a new bandaid," and begins to stand up from the sofa.
Ross stands up and walks toward the hallway behind the sofa.
Ross stops at the hallway entrance, turns back, and asks, "Hey, how about the time I cut the legs off your Malibu Ken?" Monica quickly leans forward and says, "That was you?"
Ross steps back toward the living room, facing Monica from the hallway entrance.
Ross looks at Monica, holding his arm, and says, "They were infected. He wouldn't have made it."
Monica turns toward the apartment door and smiles. Joey enters carrying a baby strapped to his chest in a carrier, followed by Chandler holding a folded stroller.
Monica walks toward the door and says, "My little nephew. Come here, little one."
Joey and Chandler step further into the room. Monica stops in front of Joey.
Monica bends down toward the baby in the carrier and says, "There's my little baby."
Joey begins unbuckling the baby carrier straps.
Monica says, "My little boy," as Joey lifts the baby out of the carrier and hands him to her.
Monica holds the baby in her arms. Joey and Chandler look at the baby, their expressions tense.
Monica smiles at the baby and says, "Hey, he's not crying."
Joey and Chandler look at each other, their facial tension relaxing into slight smiles.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Hey, he's not crying," his voice upbeat.
Monica continues holding the baby close. Joey and Chandler both grin and pump their fists in a celebratory gesture.
Chandler says, "Yes," while he and Joey continue to pump their fists.
Joey and Chandler turn and walk toward the kitchen table, carrying the baby items.
Chandler says, "there's still" as he approaches the table with the stroller.
Joey and Chandler set the baby items on the table and nearby chairs. Ross walks out of the hallway toward Monica.
Ross takes the baby from Monica, holds the baby against his shoulder, and says, "how's my little, huh? You want Daddy to change you now?"
Ross holds the baby securely and says, "I know. So did" while Chandler and Joey stand by the kitchen counter.
Ross walks to an armchair, lays the baby down, and asks, "you have fun with Uncle Joey and Uncle Chandler today?"
Joey smiles and replies, "Oh, yeah. He rode the bus today," while Chandler stands beside him with his arms crossed.
Ross leans over the armchair, unfastening the baby's clothing. Joey and Chandler watch with slight smiles.
Ross looks down at the baby and says, "Well, big boy riding the bus."
Ross pulls off the baby's pants, then lifts the baby up and looks at the baby's back.
Holding the baby, Ross says, "Hey, I have a question."
Ross turns to face Joey and Chandler, holding the baby with its back facing them.
Ross asks, "How come it says Property of Human Services on his butt?" Joey and Chandler's smiles disappear.
Joey and Chandler exchange a quick look. Chandler raises a finger, pointing at Joey.
Chandler points at Joey and says, "You".
Chandler lowers his hand, then points at Joey again. Joey points his thumb back at Chandler.
Chandler points at Joey and says, "are going to love this."
Joey forces a smile, looking toward Ross and Monica.
Joey steps toward Monica, gestures toward the baby, and says, "Can you hold him for a sec." Monica reaches out toward Ross to take the baby.
Monica takes the baby from Ross. Joey turns sharply toward the apartment door.
Joey gestures toward Chandler and says, "Come here. Come here."
Chandler grabs a pie from the table, holds it out in front of him, and says, "Stay back. I've got kiwi. Run, Joey, run." Joey bolts out the door, with Chandler running right behind him.
The screen turns black.
White text on the black screen reads: "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN DAVID CRANE".

Inside a coffee shop, Stephanie and Phoebe sit on an orange couch, facing each other slightly. Both hold acoustic guitars, and several patrons are visible in the background. Stephanie strums her guitar while Phoebe watches.
Stephanie continues playing her guitar. Phoebe raises her hand slightly and speaks, prompting Stephanie to stop playing.
Phoebe says, "No, I'm sorry." She plays her guitar and sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat,". Stephanie keeps her gaze on Phoebe.
Stephanie looks down at her guitar and plays a chord progression, maintaining her focus on her hands.
Stephanie sings, "smelly cat, smelly cat" while playing her guitar. Phoebe nods and asks, "better?"
Stephanie looks at Phoebe and says, "Yeah, much better." Phoebe replies, "Good. And you know what? Don't feel bad, because it's a hard song."
Stephanie nods slightly and says, "Yeah,".
Both women remain seated in silence, their hands resting on their guitars.
Phoebe asks, "you want to try it again?" Stephanie replies, "Yeah."
Stephanie says, "From the top."
Phoebe shakes her head briefly and says, "Okay. There is no top, all right?"
Stephanie nods slightly, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe adds, "That's the beauty of smelly cat".
Stephanie continues to nod, keeping her gaze fixed on Phoebe.
Phoebe asks, "Why don't you just follow me?" Stephanie responds, "Okay."
Phoebe positions her hand on the guitar neck and prepares to play.
Phoebe plays her guitar and sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat".
Stephanie joins in, overlapping Phoebe's vocals with a higher pitch. They sing, "what are they? You smell" while both play their guitars.
The women continue strumming their guitars.
Stephanie sings, "it?" holding the vocal note.
Phoebe sings, "Smelly cat" while Stephanie simultaneously sings, "it's not your", adding vocal fluctuations. Both continue playing.
Phoebe stops playing her guitar and looks directly at Stephanie. Stephanie stops playing shortly after, maintaining eye contact.
Phoebe says, "too much." Stephanie looks down and says, "Sorry."
The screen turns black.
The screen remains black until the end of the segment.
